title: >=2 indicator trend explainers (education only, guidance pointers)
context: Following the foundational 'how to read a screening statistic' explainer (task 014), this task produces explainers for at least two specific screening indicators. Each explainer interprets what the trend data for that indicator shows — what it does and does not say — attributes guideline context to the appropriate authoritative bodies (USPSTF, ACS, national programs) with citation and version/date, and links to the verified support/helpline pointers (task 016). These are education only, never advice. Every figure must link back to its source in the M1/M2 dataset. Both oncologist and patient-advocate sign-offs are blocking prerequisites. Cannot start until oncology clinician AND patient-advocate reviewers are secured.
objective: Write plain-language educational trend explainers for at least two screening indicators (e.g., breast/mammography and one other). Each explainer must explain what the trend does and does not say, attribute guideline context to authoritative bodies with citation and version/date, link every figure to its dataset provenance, link verified support/helpline pointers, carry the 'not medical advice' notice, and pass the full misread checklist. Both oncologist and advocate sign-offs are blocking.
acceptanceCriteria: ENTRY GATE: oncology clinician AND patient-advocate reviewers both secured (named, confirmed) before this task starts; task is blocked until both are in place; foundational explainer (task 014) must be published first
At least two indicator trend explainers produced (e.g., breast/mammography and one of cervical, colorectal, or lung); each clearly scoped to its indicator and population denominator

Each explainer explains what the trend does and does not say: what a change in coverage rate means at the population level, what it does not imply about causes, and what it does not say about any individual's situation

Guideline context attributed to USPSTF, ACS, or relevant national bodies with citation, version, and date as education about existing guidelines — never restated as the project's own advice; framed as 'USPSTF recommends...' not 'you should...'
Every figure and statistic cited is provenance-linked to its specific series in the M1/M2 dataset; no unsourced claims appear
Verified support/helpline pointers (from task 016) linked in each explainer
Persistent 'This is general information, not medical advice — consult your care team' notice present on every explainer
Full misread checklist passed for each explainer: denominator stated, basis labeled, model-based flagged, no causal verbs, no advice verbs, guideline body cited with version/date
WCAG 2.2 AA and plain-language readability target met for each explainer
Oncologist sign-off recorded for each explainer (clinical accuracy, no advice-creep) — BLOCKING
Patient-advocate sign-off recorded for each explainer (comprehension, tone, patient-serving framing) — BLOCKING
output: At least two published plain-language educational indicator trend explainers: misread checklist passed, every figure provenance-linked to the M1/M2 dataset, guideline context attributed to authoritative bodies with citation/version/date, verified support pointers linked, 'not medical advice' notice present, WCAG 2.2 AA met; both oncologist and patient-advocate sign-offs recorded (blocking) for each explainer.
